channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Almost a million ConnectOnCall users may have had data stolen by hackers
item.description: 900,000 patients who called doctors out of hours may have had their information stolen following data breach.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How Anyscale cofounder Robert Nishihara is smoothing AI’s complexities
item.description: As businesses reinvent workflows and products with AI, many such initiatives have stalled or failed to reach production. It can be really hard to implement the technology.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Google and Microsoft aren’t syncing up: why Phone Link might no longer show you sensitive notifications after installing Android 15
item.description: Android 15's new privacy rules hide sensitive notifications, impacting Windows Phone Link users who installed Link to Windows manually from the Play Store.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 2025 is the year to unlock the power of pervasive IT governance
item.description: In IDC’s April 2024 CIO Poll Survey of 105 senior IT professionals and CIOs, “developing better IT governance and enterprise architecture” emerged as one of the top priorities for 2024, ranking fourth.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Blood in the iPhones? Apple faces criminal charges
item.description: Apple has been accused of knowingly financing a trade characterized by atrocities in criminal litigation launched by the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC). It relates to use of so-called “blood minerals” in its devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Equinix to end its bare-metal service
item.description: Hosting and colocation giant Equinix is sunsetting its bare-metal infrastructure-as-a-service (IaaS) offering by June 2026. New features will no longer be added to Equinix Metal, as the service was called.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Data Center Designs Aimed at Addressing AI Energy Demands and Sustainability Challenges
item.description: Schneider Electric introduces AI-ready data center designs to address energy demands and sustainability challenges brought by artificial intelligence. The new solutions aim to enhance efficiency, sustainability, and scalability for AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Smarter devops: How to avoid deployment horrors
item.description: Many devops organizations implement advanced CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and other automations to increase deployment frequency. In the State of DevOps Report 2023, 18% of respondents were classified as elite performers for being able to deploy on-demand with change lead times of less than a day.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How data scientist Rebecca Portnoff is pushing AI companies to tackle child abuse head on
item.description: As she was finishing up her undergraduate degree at Princeton University, Rebecca Portnoff was trying to figure out what was next: more school, or go work for a tech company? She studied computer science, doing her dissertation on natural language processing.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Making AI popular is a marathon, not a sprint
item.description: AI is everywhere, but from where I sit, there are some strong signals that suggest the road to AI Everywhere is going to be a long-distance endurance race, rather than a sprint.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The case for private clouds for data management in government
item.description: When it comes to taking advantage of AI, storing data in a cost-effective, massively scalable private or hybrid cloud solution is often the best choice. The post The case for private clouds for data management in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: This Mark Cuban-backed startup is helping creators monetize their audiences
item.description: Falon Fatemi first met Mark Cuban in 2011 at South By Southwest, where the two bonded over ideas about untapped opportunities in the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he has a 'warm spot' in his heart for TikTok as app faces ban
item.description: President-elect Trump said Monday that he has a “warm spot” in his heart for TikTok as the popular social media app faces down a potential ban next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Eddie AI is one of the best video editing tools out there
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: How to upgrade to the Hyte Y70 PC case to give your parts more breathing room
item.description: The Hyte Y70 is a stylish PC case that will help PC gamers get the most out of their graphics cards and other high-end parts
item.dc:creator: Gieson Cacho, The Mercury News

item.title: Google, Microsoft could turn gatekeepers for advanced AI chips
item.description: The US government could designate cloud providers including Google and Microsoft as global gatekeepers for advanced AI chips, a step widely viewed as the latest effort to block China’s access to this critical technology, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to factory reset your Android
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how to securely back up and factory reset your Android to protect your privacy and data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What tech teams need to know about WebAssembly
item.description: WebAssembly, or Wasm, is an open standard that defines a portable binary-code format and corresponding text format for executable programs. Created and maintained by the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C), Wasm is designed to facilitate high-performance applications on web pages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI-powered deception: The sneaky macOS malware masquerading as your next video call
item.description: Artificial intelligence is making life easier for cybercriminals, allowing them to create elaborate scams to trick people. Kurt the Cyberguy explains how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to delete multiple emails at once on Android
item.description: Cut through all the digital clutter and delete multiple emails from your Android simultaneously. Kurt the CyberGuy explains how it's done.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NJ senator says mysterious drones 'almost certainly planes,' slams federal response
item.description: Sen. Andy Kim (D-N.J.) said mysterious drones hovering over the Garden State are likely planes in a Saturday series of posts on the social platform X as public concern mounts over the sightings.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Some software stocks may be even less desirable in a rebounding market
item.description: Some recent developments suggest the beleaguered software group of stocks may be coming back into favor, as I noted a couple weeks ago following positive responses to both Snowflake and Elastic.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Smart cities in action: Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV project sets a new standard
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Enterprise buyer’s guide: How to choose videoconferencing software
item.description: When most businesspeople think of videoconferencing software, the first thing that comes to mind is probably Microsoft Teams or Zoom, but there are many other choices, each with its own strengths.
item.dc:creator: us

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KREX 5
